agent_invocations_validation.violation_message:
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════
❌ PROTOCOL VIOLATION: Agent invocation incomplete
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

Expected: 3 agent invocations
Actual: 2 completed invocations

Missing phases:
  - Phase 3 (Implementation)

Cannot generate completion report until all agents are invoked.
Review the AGENT INVOCATIONS LOG above to see which phases were skipped.

AGENT INVOCATIONS LOG:
✅ Phase 4 (Phase 4): test-orchestrator (completed in ?s)
✅ Phase 5 (Phase 5): code-reviewer (completed in ?s)

TASK WILL BE MOVED TO BLOCKED STATE
Reason: Protocol violation - required agents not invoked
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════

completion_promises.criterion_text: When the autobuild_runner's _update_state writes lifecycle="awaiting_approval", a pipeline.build-paused.<feature_id> envelope is published with the build's correlation_id.
completion_promises.evidence: Added LifecycleEmitterAdapter class to src/forge/subagents/autobuild_runner.py with LIFECYCLE_TO_PIPELINE_EMIT routing table mapping 'awaiting_approval' -> 'emit_paused'. The adapter implements SubagentEmitter Protocol, schedules emit_paused on the running event loop, and threads the BuildContext.correlation_id onto the envelope. Verified by TestPausePublishAwaitingApproval::test_update_state_awaiting_approval_publishes_build_paused which asserts subject == 'pipeline.build-paused.FEAT-X' and envelope.correlation_id == 'corr-001'.

completion_promises.criterion_text: When the approval subscriber matches an approval response with the build's correlation_id, a pipeline.build-resumed.<feature_id> envelope is published before the orchestrator advances.
completion_promises.evidence: Added optional lifecycle_emitter, build_context, expected_correlation_id parameters to ApprovalSubscriber.await_response. Added per-build _resume_publish_ctx mapping populated on await_response and consumed in _on_envelope. When a first-arrival valid response is enqueued, the subscriber awaits emitter.emit_resumed() BEFORE queue.put(payload). Verified by TestResumePublishOnApproval::test_first_arrival_publishes_build_resumed and test_resume_published_before_orchestrator_advances which assert publish ordering precedes queue dequeue.

completion_promises.criterion_text: An approval response with a mismatched correlation_id does NOT publish build-resumed; the build stays paused (Group E security scenario; DDR-001 / ASSUM-016).
completion_promises.evidence: Added correlation-id allowlist check in ApprovalSubscriber._on_envelope (Step 2b): when expected_correlation_id is registered for the build and envelope.correlation_id mismatches, the subscriber logs WARNING and returns early — emit_resumed is NOT called and the response is not enqueued. Verified by TestMismatchedCorrelationIdRejected::test_mismatched_correlation_does_not_publish_resumed.

completion_promises.criterion_text: After a daemon restart while a build is paused, the build re-enters paused state, re-emits build-paused, and re-publishes the approval request with the same correlation_id (Group D scenario; ADR-ARCH-021).
completion_promises.evidence: Verified by tests/integration/test_pause_resume_e2e.py::TestPauseResumeRoundTrip::test_pause_restart_resume_round_trip which simulates a daemon restart by re-instantiating LifecycleEmitterAdapter with the SAME emitter+ctx and calling _update_state(awaiting_approval) on the rehydrated state. Asserts that two build-paused publishes occur and the second envelope's correlation_id == CORRELATION_ID (verbatim reuse, ADR-ARCH-021). Existing recover_paused_builds in src/forge/gating/wrappers.py already handles approval-request re-emission with the persisted request_id (R5 closure).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Only the first approval response wins (idempotent first-wins from FEAT-FORGE-004; not new behaviour but exercised by the test).
completion_promises.evidence: The existing _check_and_record dedup buffer in ApprovalSubscriber arbitrates duplicates under asyncio.Lock. The new emit_resumed call is gated AFTER dedup so a second matching response is dropped before reaching the publish path. Verified by TestFirstResponseWins::test_second_matching_response_is_dedup_dropped (sequential dedup) and test_only_first_approval_advances_orchestrator in the e2e suite (concurrent dedup via asyncio.gather on two identical envelopes). Both assert exactly one build-resumed publish and one queue entry.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Publish failures on emit_build_paused / emit_build_resumed log at WARNING and do not regress SQLite state.
completion_promises.evidence: LifecycleEmitterAdapter._safe_run wraps every scheduled coroutine in try/except, logging at WARNING (DDR-007 §Failure-mode contract). ApprovalSubscriber._on_envelope wraps the emit_resumed call in try/except, logging at WARNING; the queue still receives the payload so the orchestrator can resume even on a transient publish hiccup. PipelineLifecycleEmitter._safe_publish (existing) already swallows PublishFailure. Verified by TestPublishFailureContract::test_publish_failure_on_pause_logs_warning and test_publish_failure_on_resume_logs_warning — both assert state continues, queue receives, and WARNING/ERROR is logged.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors.
completion_promises.evidence: Lint/format was not run as part of this turn; the AutoBuildOrchestrator runs the lint gate in Phase 4. Code follows existing module conventions (PEP 8, type hints, snake_case, 88-char line target via Black, docstrings on all public symbols). New code in autobuild_runner.py and approval_subscriber.py mirrors the surrounding style.
